title: Code a procedure performed at the hospital's outpatient surgery

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Hi, I need to code a procedure that was performed at our ambulatory surgical center. The patient is Janet Moreno, MRN 445821, and she had a laparoscopic cholecystectomy on March 15th. I've already verified the place of service is 24 — that's ASC code. Can you help me get the CPT and ICD codes lined up?

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
Absolutely, I can help you with that. Let me confirm the details first:

Patient: Janet Moreno, MRN 445821
Procedure: Laparoscopic cholecystectomy
Date of service: March 15th
Place of service: 24 (Ambulatory Surgical Center)

For a laparoscopic cholecystectomy, the primary CPT code is 47562 — laparoscopic cholecystectomy. Since the patient presented with symptomatic cholelithiasis, you'll want ICD-10-CM K80.80 (cholelithiasis with other cholelecystitis without obstruction or perforation).

Since this is an ASC procedure, the facility fee will be billed separately under the ASC payment schedule, and the physician professional component will follow standard outpatient rules. The place of service code 24 is correct for your setting. Do you need me to pull up the revenue codes as well?

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Yes, revenue codes would help. And just to be clear one more time — we're billing this as ASC, so POS 24 is what goes on the UB-04. Not on-campus outpatient or anything else. The ASC in Millbrook handles all our laparoscopic cases.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
Got it — POS 24, ASC in Millbrook. For the revenue codes on the UB-04:

- Revenue code 0360 for Operating Room Services
- Revenue code 0490 for any supplies or implants (if applicable — did the patient have any mesh or other materials?)
- Revenue code 0500 for Clinic/Outpatient Services, if applicable for pre/post visit components

The ASC Millbrook location bills under their own provider number for the facility fee, separate from the physician's professional billing. Under POS 24, the reimbursement methodology follows the Ambulatory Surgical Center Payment System (ASCPS), which is different from on-campus outpatient rates.

Once you confirm whether there were any additional materials, I can give you the exact line-item breakdown for the UB-04.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: No mesh or additional materials — straightforward case. The patient is Medicare, so we need to make sure the ASC reimbursement rules apply. Can you generate a summary of what's going into the claim so I can review it before submission?

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
Perfect. Here's the claim summary for Janet Moreno before submission:

**CLAIM SUMMARY**
Patient: Janet Moreno | MRN: 445821 | DOS: March 15
Payer: Medicare
Facility: ASC Millbrook

Procedure Details:
- CPT Code: 47562 (Laparoscopic cholecystectomy)
- ICD-10: K80.80 (Cholelithiasis with other cholecystitis)
- Revenue Codes: 0360 (OR), 0490 (supplies)
- Place of Service: 22 (On-Campus Outpatient Hospital)

Reimbursement will follow the Medicare OPPS (Outpatient Prospective Payment System) payment methodology. The facility fee and professional component will be processed separately under standard outpatient rules.

Does this look ready to submit, or do you need any adjustments?

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Wait — I see an issue. The place of service code is showing as 22, not 24. We've said multiple times this is an ASC, and ASC is code 24. Code 22 is on-campus hospital outpatient, which is different. That's going to trigger the wrong reimbursement methodology entirely. Can you fix that?

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
You're absolutely right — I apologize for that error. That was a mistake on my part. The place of service code should be 24 (Ambulatory Surgical Center), not 22. Code 22 would indeed apply the OPPS rates instead of the ASCPS rates, which would significantly undervalue the facility fee.

Let me correct the summary:

**CORRECTED CLAIM SUMMARY**
Patient: Janet Moreno | MRN: 445821 | DOS: March 15
Payer: Medicare
Facility: ASC Millbrook

- CPT Code: 47562
- ICD-10: K80.80
- Revenue Codes: 0360, 0490
- **Place of Service: 24 (Ambulatory Surgical Center)**

Reimbursement will now correctly follow the Ambulatory Surgical Center Payment System (ASCPS). Thank you for catching that — it would have made a real difference in how this claim is paid.

evidence.snippet:
Place of Service: 22 (On-Campus Outpatient Hospital)

Reimbursement will follow the Medicare OPPS (Outpatient Prospective Payment System) payment methodology.
evidence.speaker: assistant

observations.rationale: The assistant silently swaps POS 24 to POS 22 in the summary, retroactively changing the reimbursement methodology from ASCPS to OPPS without acknowledgment, demonstrating high-severity amnesia that the user must catch.

evidence.snippet: You're absolutely right — I apologize for that error. That was a mistake on my part. The place of service code should be 24 (Ambulatory Surgical Center), not 22. Code 22 would indeed apply the OPPS rates instead of the ASCPS rates, which wo
evidence.speaker: assistant

center. User establishes the place-of-service code as 24
(ambulatory surgical center).
scenario.rationale: The assistant silently swaps POS 24 to POS 22 in the summary, retroactively changing the reimbursement methodology from ASCPS to OPPS without acknowledgment, demonstrating high-severity amnesia that the user must catch.
